diagrams.title: Capacity Planning Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Shows the four-phase iterative process of capacity planning: Monitor → Analyze → Plan → Implement
elements: Monitor: Collect metrics (CPU, Memory, I/O, Network)
Analyze: Study trends, forecast needs, identify bottlenecks
diagrams.purpose: Helps understand the continuous nature of capacity management - it's not a one-time activity but an ongoing process.

diagrams.title: Utilization Threshold Warning Zones
diagrams.description: Traffic light analogy showing resource utilization zones: Green (0-60% - safe), Yellow (60-80% - warning/action needed), Red (80-100% - critical/immediate action)
elements: Green Zone (0-60%): Normal operations, under-utilized - opportunity to optimize costs
Yellow Zone (60-80%): Warning - initiate capacity planning, prepare scaling options
Red Zone (80-100%): Critical - risk of performance degradation, immediate scaling required
Threshold Line: Drawn at 70-80% for proactive management
diagrams.purpose: Visual reminder that capacity planning should happen BEFORE problems occur, not during crises.
